title: Trajectory Definition
description: A Tier 2-4 Cartesian trajectory: trajectory_manifest.csv metadata plus an ordered sequence of timestamped end-effector waypoints reconstructed from the generated trajectories/<type>/<trajectory_id>.npz. Position unit: meter. Time unit: second. Quaternion order: wxyz.

trajectory_id.description: Unique identifier for this trajectory, matching trajectory_manifest.csv.

orientation_mode.description: Whether end-effector orientation is held fixed or varies (SO(3) geodesic interpolation) along the trajectory.

closed_path.description: Whether the trajectory returns to its starting pose (closed loop).

duration_s.description: Total nominal duration of the trajectory in seconds.

control_period_s.description: Realized time step between consecutive waypoints, in seconds (duration_s / (num_waypoints - 1)).

generation_status.description: 'validated' means every waypoint passed the sequential warm-started DLS reachability check at generation time; this is a data-generation check, not a Tier 2 evaluation result.

waypoints.description: Ordered list of waypoints comprising the trajectory.

waypoint_id.description: Zero-based waypoint index, unique and strictly increasing within a trajectory.

time_s.description: Timestamp of this waypoint in seconds from trajectory start; strictly increasing across waypoints.

path_parameter_s.description: Quintic time-scaled path parameter s(tau) in [0, 1]; non-decreasing across waypoints.

target_position.description: End-effector position [x, y, z] in meters, world frame.

target_quaternion.description: End-effector orientation as a unit quaternion [w, x, y, z] (wxyz order), world frame.

target_linear_velocity.description: Analytic Cartesian linear velocity [vx, vy, vz] in m/s, consistent with the quintic time scaling.

target_linear_acceleration.description: Analytic Cartesian linear acceleration [ax, ay, az] in m/s^2, consistent with the quintic time scaling.
